regimeSub: OPEC+ output expansion hammers Brent to $72.56 as post-NFP stagflation narrative meets oil deflation.
themes: OPEC+ Jul 6 output expansion + Iran crude returning to pre-war levels → Brent collapses to $72.56, easing energy-driven CPI pressure but undercutting stagflation inflation leg
June NFP 57K vs ~150K est with LFPR at 5-year low persists → Warsh's 4-week decision window now priced as hold not hike, DXY slips to 100.86
BOJ at 1% + tapering end FY2027 still fails to arrest USDJPY at 161.89 → carry trade intact as Japan real rates stay deeply negative vs US 4.2% CPI / 3.63% policy
KOSPI retreats to 7558 from 7909 as ₩148T foreign outflows accelerate → BOK Jul 16 hike speculation (US jobs shock divergence) fails to support KRW at 1525

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = -57bp real rate; negative real rates persist despite Warsh hawk rhetoric at Sintra

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2s 4.14% / 10s 4.49% = +35bp steepening; curve normalizing as growth fears outweigh near-term hike risk

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: VIX 15.57 implies HY spreads remain tight; credit complacent despite labor crack in June NFP

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX at 15.57 down from 16.15 ytd; vol compression continues despite stagflation crosscurrents

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $72.56 post-OPEC+ expansion; pre-Iran war levels signal energy inflation impulse fading

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4151 holds despite worst monthly drop since 2008 in June; haven bid persists on growth scare

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: USDKRW 1525 vs 1533 yesterday; KRW stabilizing as BOK Jul 16 hike odds rise on US-Korea divergence

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: KOSPI 7558 (-4.4% from 7909) as SK Hynix/Samsung retreat; HBM euphoria meets foreign ₩148T outflows

headline: Oil normalizes to pre-Iran war levels as OPEC+ expands output, complicating Warsh's July FOMC calculus as stagflation's inflation leg weakens while labor market crack persists.
